## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On November 13, 1985, a massive, incredibly terrifying, and deeply catastrophic natural disaster aggressively struck Colombia. The Nevado del Ruiz volcano suffered an incredibly massive, explosive eruption. The massive, superheated pyroclastic flows violently melted the mountain's glaciers, aggressively sending massive, horrific lahars (mudflows) hurtling down the valleys. The massive wall of mud and debris absolutely completely annihilated the town of Armero, resulting in the incredibly tragic, horrific deaths of over 23,000 people, becoming the absolute deadliest volcanic disaster of the 20th century.

On November 13, 1940, the incredibly massive, uniquely pioneering, and culturally defining animated film *Fantasia* was officially and aggressively released by Walt Disney. The massive, highly ambitious masterpiece radically and heavily combined classical music with incredibly massive, groundbreaking animation. The incredibly massive financial gamble absolutely revolutionized the cinematic experience by heavily introducing Fantasound, the absolute, very first massive commercial use of stereophonic sound in motion pictures, fundamentally and permanently changing the massive technological landscape of global cinema.

On November 13, 1780, Maharaja Ranjit Singh, the incredibly massive, absolutely brilliant, and universally revered "Lion of Punjab," was officially born in Gujranwala (present-day Pakistan). He achieved absolute, historical immortality by heavily unifying the deeply fractured Sikh misls to aggressively forge the incredibly massive, incredibly powerful Sikh Empire.
